title: ranunculus parviflorus subsp. incrassatus
intro: Ranunculus parviflorus subsp. incrassatus is a lesser-known subspecies of the small-flowered buttercup, a perennial plant native to North America. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus parviflorus subsp. incrassatus include its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's root and leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity.
